items.title: A First-Ever Default Shakes an $80 Billion Corner of Muni Market
items.summary: More than two decades after Wall Street started pumping out a new type of bonds — those backed by the legal-settlement payments governments receive from cigarette companies — one batch has finally been driven into a default. It almost certainly won’t be the last.

items.title: Federal Reserve holds first meeting of Kevin Warsh era

items.title: A primer on uranium enrichment as Iran nuclear program faces scrutiny – Winnipeg Free Press
US Oil Stockpiles Fall to Critical Lows as War Spurs Exports
items.summary: Inventories at the US’s largest commercial crude oil storage hub fell to the lowest since 2014, as surging exports during the Iran war drain supplies in America.

items.title: ‘Humiliation’: Trump battles claims his Iran deal is worse than Obama’s
items.summary: US president’s critics ask if concessions to Tehran in purported agreement were worth four months of war

items.title: Federal Reserve holds rates steady as Kevin Warsh chairs his first meeting - and now a rate hike is looking more likely than a cut this year
Fed Getting US Ready for Rate Hikes, JPMorgan's Michele Says
items.summary: Bob Michele, JPMorgan Asset Management's global head of fixed income, says the Federal Reserve's policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee is showing a hawkish tilt after its decision to leave rates unchanged. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance: The Fed Decides." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US Yields Jump as Fed Dot Plot Boosts Trader Bets on a 2026 Rate Hike
items.summary: US Treasury yields leaped as Federal Reserve officials signaled they expect an interest-rate hike in the coming months, pushing traders to fully price in higher borrowing costs in 2026.

items.title: Dollar Rallies as Fed Officials Open Door to 2026 Rate Hike
items.summary: The dollar surged the most in three months after Federal Reserve officials signaled growing support for interest-rate hikes this year.

items.summary: Kevin Warsh has eliminated explicit forward guidance, but his colleagues project that at least one rate rise is needed

items.title: SpaceX Fuels AI Boom Higher | Open Interest 6/17/2026

items.title: Fed's Warsh 'Destined to Disappoint' Trump, JPM's Michele
items.summary: "We have to accept the economy is in pretty good shape," Bob Michele, JPMorgan Asset Management's global head of fixed income, says during an interview on "Bloomberg Surveillance: The Fed Decides." (Source: Bloomberg)
